mentions.raw_name: Aegon the Dragon / Aegon the Conqueror
Theon's yew bow and quiver of arrows
Aegon the Conqueror / Aegon the Dragon (historical)
Sons of the River King (historical, unnamed)
Priest of the Drowned God leading horses (this is Aeron before identified)
Summer Isles (referenced as source of feathers)

Drowned God (ironborn faith; Aeron is priest; seawater blessing ritual; "What is dead may never die, but rises again, harder and stronger"; seaweed in hair; the Drowned God "brought flame from the sea")
The Faith of the Seven (sept at Lordsport destroyed and never rebuilt; "the new gods" whose taste was soured by Robert's fury)
Old Gods / wolf gods (Aeron asks if Theon prays to "wolf gods"; Ned Stark "prayed to a tree")
Ironborn / Ironmen (extensively described: the Old Way, salt wives, rock wives, reaving, iron price vs. gold price, mining, fishing, farming; "hard places breed hard men")
Rivermen / weakling rivermen (Aegon gave Harren's kingdom to them)
Greyjoy banner (golden kraken on black field)
Great Kraken (Balon's ship, grey iron ram bow)
Black iron breastplate and pothelm (guard's armor)

mentions.raw_name: The red comet (visible in sky; interpreted variously: Mallisters argue its meaning; Theon claims it as his; Aeron says it is a burning brand from the Drowned God proclaiming a rising tide)
Greyjoy Rebellion (Balon's rebellion — referenced extensively: Robert besieged Pyke, Ned Stark at his side; Rodrik killed at Seagard; Maron crushed; south tower collapsed; Theon taken as hostage)
War of the Five Kings (current: Robb crowned King in the North, broken with Iron Throne, marching west; Kingslayer captive; Tywin at Harrenhal)
King of Salt and Rock (Balon claims)
Son of the Sea Wind (Balon claims)
Hand of the King (not explicitly named but implied in political structure)
